nativeRange.habitat: Collected from the Lake Sulawesi area of Indonesia. Its specific micro-habitat has not been formally documented, but the genus Ammannia is broadly associated with shallow, wet or marshy margins of lakes, ponds, and slow-moving freshwater bodies.

decorPreferences: high light to develop orange/pink/red coloration
stable parameters — sensitive to swings during emersed-to-submersed transition

summary: Ammannia Sp. Sulawesi is a compact red-to-orange stem plant collected from the Lake Sulawesi region of Indonesia. It has no formal species description and is closely related to Ammannia gracilis, sharing similar care demands but staying noticeably shorter and tending toward warm orange-pink tones under high light and CO2.
careNotes:
Ammannia sp. 'Sulawesi' is a stem plant collected from the Lake Sulawesi area of Indonesia that has no formal species description — the 'sp.' notation reflects that it is known only at the genus level in the hobby trade. It is closely related to Ammannia gracilis (native to tropical West Africa) and Ammannia senegalensis, and is described by specialty retailers as more compact than A. gracilis, typically reaching 10–20 cm in height compared to the 20–50 cm commonly attributed to gracilis. Its colour palette runs from green through orange to pink and red depending on conditions, rather than the deeper copper-red typical of gracilis, and it is generally considered slightly less demanding, though it is still best suited to hobbyists with stable, mature, well-fertilised tanks.

Water parameters that are broadly recommended fall in the range of 22–28 °C, pH 6.0–7.5, and soft-to-moderately-hard water. To develop the characteristic orange-pink coloration, the plant generally requires high light, pressurised CO2, and consistent dosing of iron alongside macro and micro nutrients; under lower light it tends to remain greener and less vibrant. A nutrient-rich substrate — such as aquasoil or a standard substrate supplemented with root tabs — is preferred, and regular trimming encourages lateral side-shoot growth that keeps the plant bushy and full in a mid-to-background position. Like other Ammannia species, it can be sensitive to sudden shifts in water chemistry, particularly during the transition from emersed (grown above water) to submersed (grown fully underwater) growth, so stable conditions and gradual acclimation are commonly advised when introducing new stems.
breedingNotes: Ammannia Sp. Sulawesi is propagated by stem cuttings: trimming the upper portion of a healthy stem — roughly 5–10 cm with several leaves intact — and replanting it into the substrate is the standard method. Trimming established stems also encourages the plant to produce lateral side shoots, resulting in a bushier, more compact form over time. Seed or flower-based propagation has not been documented for this regional form.

primary.name: Aqua Lab Aquaria
primary.notes: Promoted to primary because the manifest-supplied Buce Plant URL (https://buceplant.com/products/ammania-sp-sulawesi) returned HTTP 404. Used for adult height (4-8+ in / 10-20+ cm), light (high), CO2 (beneficial/recommended), growth rate (moderate), placement (midground/background), difficulty (intermediate), origin (Lake Sulawesi, Indonesia), and colour (green/red, paling to orange).

additional.name: Ultum Nature Systems
additional.notes: Cross-checked origin (Indonesia), difficulty (intermediate; not overly difficult but recommended for experienced hobbyists with mature, balanced tanks), placement (midground-to-background), lighting (moderate-to-high), CO2 (required), nutrient dosing needed, and orange-green-pink colour palette.

additional.name: Buce Plant (via web search snippet)
additional.notes: Manifest's intended primary; direct fetch returned 404. Content extracted via web-search snippet only: confirms temperature 72-82 °F (22-28 °C), pH 6.0-7.5, soft-to-moderate hardness, CO2 injection + moderate-to-high lighting + quality nutrient dosing, regular trimming for compact bushy shape, propagation by stem cuttings, and difficulty 'not particularly difficult but recommended for those experienced with finicky stems'. Origin Lake Sulawesi, Indonesia.

additional.name: Aquatic Plant Man
additional.notes: Cross-checked colour (red hue with orange around the leaf), midground placement, and 'fairly easy' difficulty (tilts slightly lower than other sources, contributing to the averaged 'intermediate' rating).

additional.name: Wikipedia — Ammannia
additional.notes: Genus-level taxonomy only (family Lythraceae, order Myrtales, subfamily Lythroideae). Wikipedia does not mention the Sulawesi regional form; this species has no formal description, hence the 'Ammannia sp.' name.
